digital-brain-skill.purpose: Help users manage their digital brain - a structured knowledge management system for personal productivity, content creation, relationship management, and goal tracking.

module.name: identity
module.purpose: Personal brand, voice, and core values

module.name: content
module.purpose: Content creation pipeline from ideas to publication

module.name: knowledge
module.purpose: Learning materials, bookmarks, and research notes

module.name: papers
module.purpose: Academic paper reading with narrative-driven approach

module.name: operations
module.purpose: Goals, tasks, meetings, and productivity metrics

format.name: jsonl
format.usage: Append-only logs for bookmarks, contacts, tasks, interactions
format.benefits: Preserves history, easy to parse, agent-friendly

format.name: yaml
format.usage: Structured configuration for goals, learning, profile

format.name: markdown
format.benefits: Easy to edit, rich formatting, version-controllable

operation.name: add-idea
operation.trigger: User wants to capture a content idea
operation.action: Append to content/ideas/ideas.jsonl with proper structure
operation.fields: id, title, description, tags, status, created_at, priority

operation.name: add-bookmark
operation.trigger: User shares a link or wants to save a resource
operation.fields: id, url, title, description, tags, saved_at, category

operation.name: add-contact
operation.fields: id, name, relationship, tags, met_at, last_contact, notes, links

operation.name: log-interaction
operation.trigger: User mentions communicating with a contact
operation.action: Append to network/relationships/interactions.jsonl and update last_contact
operation.fields: id, contact_id, date, type, summary, follow_up

operation.name: add-task
operation.trigger: User mentions something they need to do
operation.fields: id, title, description, status, priority, created_at, due_date, tags, related_goal

operation.name: weekly-review
operation.trigger: User asks for weekly review or summary
operation.action: Run scripts/weekly_review.py to analyze the week

operation.name: content-ideas
operation.trigger: User asks what to write about

operation.name: stale-contacts
operation.trigger: User asks who to reconnect with
operation.action: Run scripts/stale_contacts.py to find neglected relationships

operation.name: expand-idea
operation.trigger: User wants to turn an idea into a draft

operation.name: add-paper
operation.trigger: User wants to add an academic paper to reading list
operation.action: Run scripts/add_paper.py with title, URL, and metadata
operation.fields: id, title, authors, year, venue, url, tags, reading_status, main_claim

operation.name: search-papers
operation.trigger: User asks about papers or wants to find papers to read
operation.action: Run scripts/search_papers.py with filters (status, tags, keywords)

operation.name: read-paper
operation.trigger: User wants guidance on reading a paper
operation.phase1: Extract narrative: Read abstract/intro/conclusion, fill narrative section, draw Mermaid diagram (15-30 min)
operation.phase2: Verify data: Find evidence for claims, fill data table, critical thinking (1-2 hours)

operation.name: update-paper-status
operation.trigger: User completes a reading phase or finishes a paper
operation.action: Run scripts/update_paper_status.py to update status and metadata

operation.name: create-module
operation.trigger: User wants to extend the system or create a new module
operation.action: Open module_operation/MODULE_CREATION_GUIDE.md and guide through 6-phase process

phases.phase1: Requirements Analysis (30 min) - Define data model, workflow, tags
phases.phase2: Core Files Creation (2-3 hours) - README, data.jsonl, scripts
phases.phase3: Documentation (2-3 hours) - Templates, examples, quick start
phases.phase4: System Integration (1-2 hours) - Update 8 system files (CRITICAL)
phases.phase5: Cross-Module Integration (1-2 hours) - Define data flows
phases.phase6: Quality Assurance (1 hour) - Test and verify with check script

operation.files_to_update: SKILL.md, AGENT.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, EXAMPLES.md, README.md, knowledge/KNOWLEDGE.md, .claude/skills/digital-brain/skill.md, .claude/skills/digital-brain/instructions.xml

operation.name: check-module-integration
operation.trigger: User wants to verify a module is properly integrated
operation.action: Run module_operation/check_module_integration.py with module name and keyword
operation.verification: Checks that all 8 system files have sufficient references (5+ for core files, 3+ for skill files)

behavior-guidelines.guideline: Always read the identity module first to understand user's voice and values
Use append-only patterns for JSONL files - never overwrite existing entries
Generate unique IDs for new entries (format: type-XXX, e.g., idea-001, paper-YYYYMMDD-XXX)
Cross-reference between modules (e.g., link tasks to goals, content to bookmarks, ideas to papers)
Suggest proactive actions (e.g., "I noticed you haven't published in a while...")
Maintain consistent tagging across modules for better discovery
When running scripts, show output to user and explain insights
For papers, always use narrative-first approach: extract story before diving into data
When creating new modules, follow module_operation/MODULE_CREATION_GUIDE.md and verify with check_module_integration.py

context-loading.principle: Load progressively - only what's needed for the current task

on-skill-invocation.reason: Understand user's brand and voice for all interactions

on-content-task.reason: Access ideas, research materials, and papers for content creation

on-paper-task.reason: Access paper database and reading notes

on-network-task.reason: Access contact database and interaction history
